title: Spring is Here — and So Are the Density Altitude Warnings
body: With temps climbing into the 70s this week, density altitude at 18V is already nudging 6,500 ft on warm afternoons. If you're flying a heavier gross weight or a fixed-pitch prop, plan your departures for early morning when the air is cool and the runway feels 4,100 ft again. Perfect grass strip weather, though — the 14/32 is in great shape.

title: Pancake Breakfast Fly-In — April 11
body: First fly-in of 2026 is right around the corner. We're firing up the griddle on April 11th, 8–10 AM on the ramp. Fly in, drive in, or wander over from your hangar — all welcome. $10 suggested donation, kids fly free. CTAF 122.9, easy pattern, plenty of tiedowns.

title: Fuel at $6.15/gal — Still the Best Price on the Front Range
body: We updated 100LL to $6.15/gal last week. Self-serve, 24/7 credit card, no line. Compare that to $7.50+ at KBJC and KAPA and the math is pretty clear. Tell your flying club.
